rag_rules: When resolving references in AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
For implementation-level AGREE contracts, use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always validate that expressions have valid types and that referenced identifiers are resolvable.
When writing guarantees, ensure they relate visible inputs to visible outputs and avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in type scopes.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE declarations; avoid package-qualified or undefined types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Left-hand side of assign statements must be an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
anti_patterns: Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations in AGREE contracts; remove the clause if the root cause cannot be resolved.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
